name: the-hundred-years-war-on-palestine
description: Rashid Khalidi's definitive history of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict from 1917 to 2017 — a scholarly account framed as a settler colonial war, told through six key turning points, family archives, and the resilience of the Palestinian people.
useCase: Understanding the history of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, settler colonialism, the Balfour Declaration, the Nakba, the 1967 occupation, the Oslo Accords, and the role of external powers.
knownRisks: Presents a Palestinian nationalist perspective — not a neutral account
The settler colonial framework is contested by many scholars and historians
Some readers may find the thesis challenging or controversial
The book does not cover events after 2017 in detail
Personal family narratives may introduce subjective bias into the historical record
